profile.name: Jonathan Sparvath Nielsen
profile.title: Fullstack Developer
profile.summary: Fullstack web developer with 7+ years of professional experience building modern websites, scalable web applications, CMS platforms, and design systems. Both independently and as part of larger development teams.

experiences.description: I worked on a redesign of a complex and large legacy app that enables users to perform engineering calculations and reports. Part of the tasks included modernizing the design and coming up with improved UX solutions.

I led the implementation of a complete website redesign for Delogue using HubSpot CMS. I built custom templates and modules with HubL, CSS, and JavaScript, helping consolidate the CRM and website experience while giving the marketing team greater control over content management and site updates.

I worked on a project for one of Frankly’s clients, where I was responsible for architecting a monorepo setup that allowed multiple applications to share modules.
One requirement was that the modules be shareable between Next.js and Create React App, which required abstracting away the routing layer. Other responsibilities included implementing individual applications, testing, and internationalization.

I was hired to develop the frontend for a platform for medicinal cannabis patients and doctors. I worked on the “open website” canifyclinics.com, a customer universe, and a custom support platform. I also set up Sanity as a CMS, which allowed the client to add and edit sections, and create new pages. The platform and CMS support multiple languages.

I took over maintenance of Delogue’s website using a headless WordPress CMS and Gatsby frontend. Responsibilities included frontend development, Google Tag Manager implementation, performance optimization, SEO optimization, and form integrations.
Mercell provides a large ecosystem that allows buyers and suppliers to work with the tendering process online. I was hired to develop and maintain their contract management platform, RakatX.

As part of the digital team at Soundboks, I worked on developing their website, blog, and mobile app. A big part of my responsibilities was to implement a new design system using React-Native and React-Native-Web for the component library, so the same codebase could work for both mobile and web platforms. Another large project was selecting and implementing a CMS for the webshop, so content editors could create product and landing pages without the involvement of programmers.
I was responsible for developing the frontend for a B2B web app, enabling DHI’s clients to identify different countries’ legislation regarding their products’ chemicals.

Danmarks Miljøportal collects environmental data, which it delivers to the public sector. I was responsible for implementing their new website design, built using Umbraco CMS. A key requirement for the site was compliance with WCAG (Web Content Accessibility Guidelines).

I was hired by animator Jonas Elsgaard to implement the redesign of his website. As his clients are from around the world, I decided on a combination of a headless CMS and Next.js to make it possible to prerender the website and cache it on a CDN. The site makes extensive use of video, so I decided to use the Mux streaming platform so videos can start playing before the whole file has loaded.
As a student consultant for Kraftvaerk, I maintained and developed Resurs Bank’s website, including frontend development, CMS management, database management, and automated report generation.

projects.name: Kilnspace
projects.description: Full-stack web app for managing ceramic glaze recipes. Automatically calculates the chemical composition and allows users to create various experiments for developing their own glazes.
